title: 36 years, 300 luxury hotels, one stolen laptop: How a Charles Sobhraj admirer was caught
content: A 69-year-old man allegedly defrauded hotels across India for nearly three decades. He admitted to modelling his criminal lifestyle on notorious conman Charles Sobhraj. The man was arrested in Bhubaneswar after fleeing a Raipur hotel without paying a large bill. He had previously spent fifteen years in various Indian prisons for similar offenses. Police stated he viewed hotels as tourist destinations and prisons as his home stay.

title: 'Can do whatever she wishes': TMC on viral video of Mamata slapping party worker
content: Trinamool Congress MP Saugata Roy defended Mamata Banerjee after a video showed her slapping a party worker. He stated Banerjee shares a close bond and can do as she wishes. The incident occurred during a protest march over a minor's alleged rape and murder. BJP leaders criticized Banerjee's actions, calling it arrogance and a loss of mental balance. Clashes between TMC and BJP supporters also marked the protest march.

title: 'There is a deliberate plan against Sooryavanshi': Ex-India batter issues caution
content: Parthiv Patel believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will continue facing short-pitched bowling as opponents target his weakness against pace and bounce. After the youngster's modest scores against England, Patel said the tactic was evident during the IPL. He also urged India's batters to show greater patience, adapt to English conditions, and avoid rushing their innings after the third T20I collapse.

title: Australia to sell uranium to India: What the landmark deal means
content: India and Australia signed a landmark civil nuclear agreement for uranium supplies. The leaders also unveiled pacts covering defence and maritime security cooperation. Partnerships in cyber and critical technologies were also established between the nations. This agreement will diversify India's energy sources and strengthen its clean energy goals. Both countries committed to enhancing strategic coordination and interoperability of armed forces.

title: Salesforce staff 'confused' as it promotes Claude in $27.7 bn app it owns
content: Salesforce is promoting Anthropic's new Claude Tag AI agent inside Slack—the platform it bought for $27.7 billion—leaving employees confused, The Information reports. Claude Tag competes directly with Salesforce's own Slackbot and Agentforce, blurring the line between Salesforce's AI products and its partner's. With a 1% Anthropic stake and $300 million in annual token spending, Salesforce backs a rival that may not need Slack at all.

title: Telangana HC grants bail to Bandi Sanjay's son in POCSO case, sets Rs 1L bond
content: The Telangana high court granted regular bail to Bandi Bhagirath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar. He was arrested in May under the POCSO Act and later granted interim bail for exams. The case involves allegations of sexual harassment against a minor girl. Bhagirath denied charges and filed a counter-complaint alleging extortion. Both allegations and counter-allegations are currently under investigation.

title: New EPF scheme: 10 questions on contributions, withdrawals & retirement savings
content: EPF Scheme 2026: The good news is that many core features of the provident fund system remain familiar. Existing PF accounts continue, contribution rates broadly remain unchanged and accumulated balances remain protected.

title: New USCIS rules for H-1B, Green Card applicants start July 10
content: New United States Citizenship and Immigration Services rules will take effect soon. Applicants must use handwritten or properly reproduced signatures for immigration forms. Improper signatures will lead to rejection without any chance for correction. Applicants should always sign forms by hand before submitting them. This policy aims to maintain the integrity of the immigration system.

title: Kerala LIFE Mission: How the state's flagship housing scheme works
content: Kerala's LIFE Mission is transforming the lives of the homeless by providing essential housing and financial support. Aiming primarily at landless families and those with dilapidated homes, the initiative funds construction through grants that depend on project milestones. With a focus on marginalized communities, including SC, ST, and women-led households, it has successfully built over five lakh homes, positively impacting nearly twenty lakh individuals.

title: How the British royal family makes money without a regular job
content: The British monarchy's financial foundation is composed of the Sovereign Grant and a series of private estates. King Charles III alongside Prince William has revealed substantial tax contributions. The Sovereign Grant, sourced from Crown Estate profits, finances official royal functions and palace maintenance. Income is also generated through the Duchies of Lancaster and Cornwall, which serve to support the expenses of the royal family and working royals.

title: Space images reveal 5,000-mile seaweed belt across the Atlantic
content: Sargassum seaweed, once a crucial part of marine ecosystems, has now transformed into a significant coastal issue due to nutrient-rich runoff from land. This rapid growth of brown macroalgae poses serious environmental and economic challenges, threatening tourism and local livelihoods. Scientists are investigating the interplay of nutrient shifts and ocean currents to resolve this issue, emphasizing that tackling land-based pollution is vital to curb the spread of sargassum.

title: Microsoft layoffs: Here's what company is offering laid-off employees across US in severance
content: Microsoft announced significant job cuts affecting approximately 4,800 global employees this week.  Affected employees in the United States will receive generous severance packages and benefits.  These packages include extended base pay and continued stock vesting for eligible workers.  The layoffs primarily impact the sales division and the Xbox gaming business.  This move aligns with Microsoft's strategic cost reduction and AI infrastructure expansion plans.

title: Elon Musk fired him as Twitter CEO; IIT Bombay alumnus built a $2B AI startup
content: IIT Bombay alumnus Parag Agrawal has built an AI startup valued at around $2 billion, two years after stepping down as Twitter CEO following Elon Musk's acquisition of the company. Agrawal, who secured AIR 77 in JEE, studied Computer Science at IIT Bombay before earning a PhD from Stanford University. After leading Twitter as CEO, he founded Parallel Web Systems in 2023, focusing on AI infrastructure and large-scale computing technologies.

title: 'Will be one hell of a decision': Ashwin backs unchanged India XI despite Samson debate
content: Ravichandran Ashwin supports maintaining the same Indian team for the fourth T20I match. He believes this stability sends a strong message to the squad. The spinner acknowledges Sanju Samson will likely miss out again. Ashwin noted India's young batting unit struggles in different conditions. He hopes the team gains experience and improves with time.

title: 'Sack him...': Ben Stokes' post sparks fresh debate as ICC probes ECB over footage
content: Ben Stokes posted "Sack him..." on social media, reigniting retirement speculation. The International Cricket Council is investigating the England and Wales Cricket Board over footage. This footage was filmed inside the dressing room before Stokes announced his retirement. The ICC alleges the ECB breached regulations by releasing the video early. The investigation occurs as the ECB searches for a new Test captain.

title: Waterlogging, jams & destruction: 10 videos capture Monsoon chaos across India
content: Heavy monsoon rains have caused widespread waterlogging and traffic disruptions across India. Several states are under red and orange alerts for severe weather conditions. Authorities are managing rescue operations and monitoring vulnerable areas. Maharashtra and Kerala continue to battle significant rainfall impacts. The India Meteorological Department forecasts continued heavy showers in many regions.

title: What is Arthur Fery's nationality? Wimbledon 2026 star ends British-French debate
content: Arthur Fery has addressed questions about his nationality after becoming one of the biggest stories at Wimbledon 2026. The 23-year-old wildcard has enjoyed a dream run into the semi-finals, earning praise from fans across Britain. His impressive performances have also sparked discussion in France because he was born there and has French parents.

title: 'Just unfair': Ashwin slams selectors over Sanju Samson's Zimbabwe snub
content: Ravichandran Ashwin voiced strong disapproval of Sanju Samson's exclusion from the T20I squad. He believes frequent team changes create player insecurity and harm the T20 setup. Ashwin questioned the logic behind dropping Samson after a few recent poor scores. He warned that players might focus on self-preservation instead of winning matches. This decision has sparked considerable debate within Indian cricket circles.

title: Mumbai's rain has entered uncharted territory. The data tells a worrying story
content: Mumbai experienced unprecedented rainfall, exceeding normal monthly totals in just one week. This extreme weather reflects climate change altering rainfall patterns across India. Warmer air and seas contribute to more intense and concentrated rain events. Urban development and infrastructure limits exacerbate flooding issues in the city. Adapting to these changing monsoons requires smarter planning and natural buffer protection.

title: Bengaluru: PT teacher booked after 12-yr-old allegedly beaten to death with iron rod
content: A physical education trainer was booked for allegedly beating a 12-year-old student to death. The boy's father alleged the trainer used an iron rod, causing his son's death. School authorities initially informed the father the boy had fallen ill and collapsed. Parents protested at the school after learning the boy was allegedly beaten. Police are awaiting postmortem reports and collecting CCTV footage for investigation.

title: 'Welcome to the Jungle' film eyes Rs 125 crore domestic milestone before 'Dhamaal 4'
content: Ahmed Khan's multi-starrer action-comedy 'Welcome to the Jungle' wraps up its second week at the box office, closing in on the Rs. 125 crore domestic net milestone. Starring Akshay Kumar, Suniel Shetty, Arshad Warsi, and Paresh Rawal, the film stands at a domestic net of Rs. 123.06 crore ahead of its Day 14 live tracking.

title: Around 35,000 tech jobs may be eliminated in 2026, India’s IT sector sees AI shift
content: The latest trend reflects a new phase in the restructuring of India's IT services industry, which is valued at over $315 billion. Companies are continuing to rationalise the workforce built up during the pandemic hiring surge after grappling with weaker business conditions over the past three years.

title: Chilling new images reveal condition of Connor Murphy’s Thailand home before drowning death
content: Connor Murphy died in Thailand after reportedly fleeing police and entering a lake, where he later drowned. Newly released images from his rental home show trash, medication packets and damage inside the property. Police are still investigating the case and awaiting autopsy and toxicology results. Authorities have not confirmed what led to Murphy’s behavior before his death.

title: 'Was taking 18 tablets a day': Prince Narula opens up about battle with anxiety
content: Prince Narula revealed his struggle with severe anxiety and health complications. He took eighteen tablets daily and experienced significant weight gain. Music and his wife Yuvika Chaudhary provided crucial support during this difficult time. The couple faced marital challenges due to emotional exhaustion and considered parting ways. Becoming parents brought a fresh perspective, resolving conflicts by looking at their daughter.

title: 'Deeply alarming': Major scare at Gurgaon society after balcony collapses amid heavy rain
content: A luxury apartment balcony collapsed in Gurgaon after heavy rain on Thursday morning. No injuries were reported as falling debris missed residents passing below. Authorities cordoned off the area and launched an investigation into the structural safety. Residents expressed concern over builder negligence and demanded a structural audit. They urged an assessment of the entire complex for structural integrity.

title: Paid in full in 2013, car never came: Mahindra told to refund buyer with interest
content: Puducherry commission ordered Mahindra and its dealer to refund a customer. The man paid in full for a Mahindra XUV500 in 2013 but never received it. The state commission found Mahindra responsible for the vehicle's non-delivery. The dealer was also partly blamed for delays in placing the purchase order. Mahindra must refund the full amount and pay compensation to the aggrieved buyer.

title: NHL trade rumor: Penguins, Flyers eye Canucks' $92M star
content: The Vancouver Canucks have been actively looking for trade options for the team’s star center Elias Pettersson as the team rebuilds its roster, ahead of the new season. Elias Pettersson’s performance has also dropped in the last few years which has sparked some concern. Amid all the mess, it seems the Pittsburgh Penguins and the Philadelphia Flyers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Rashid Khan breaks down as Afghanistan bids emotional farewell to Shapoor Zadran
content: Afghanistan cricket bid an emotional farewell to former fast bowler Shapoor Zadran.  He passed away in India at the age of 38 after a prolonged illness.  Zadran represented Afghanistan in 44 ODIs and 36 T20Is between 2009 and 2020.  He had been undergoing treatment in New Delhi for a rare life-threatening condition.  His contributions and fighting spirit will remain an important part of cricket history.

title: 'Days, weeks or months': US gearing up for prolonged military campaign against Iran
content: The United States anticipates a prolonged military confrontation with Iran lasting weeks. This escalation follows fresh US strikes and Iran's retaliatory actions in the region. Hardline factions in Iran are reportedly frustrated with the perceived lack of benefits from a memorandum of understanding. President Donald Trump declared a ceasefire over after Iranian attacks on commercial shipping.

title: Blake Lively didn't expect this from Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce: Report
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as 1000 high profile guests belonging to Hollywood and the sports world lined up to watch the pop star walk down the aisle, sparking chaos among milllions. While the wedding had brought New York City to a standstill, it seems Blake Lively might not have been that disappointed with not being invited.

title: Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS in blockbuster World Cup final halftime show
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 final will feature a historic halftime show.  Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira, and BTS as co-headliners for this event.  Chris Martin is curating the eleven-minute performance which supports children's education.  Burna Boy and Gustavo Dudamel will also perform during the groundbreaking show.  This landmark event aims to unite sport, music, and social impact globally.

title: Stir in Mumbai's political circles as Sharad Pawar meets Eknath Shinde
content: NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar met deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde, creating political stir.  Arif Naseem Khan also held a closed-door meeting with Pawar to gauge his political mood.  Rumors of Pawar's party joining the Mahayuti are circulating among Congress insiders.  Congress is working to reclaim political space if Pawar's party shifts alliances.  Pawar's party remains a constituent of the INDIA alliance, according to Congress.

title: Exclusive: From Rajasthan to Texas - Abhimanyu Lamba's pursuit of the American dream
content: Texas Super Kings pacer Abhimanyu Lamba opens up on leaving Rajasthan to pursue cricket in the USA, overcoming homesickness, working as a part-time coach, and earning his place in the Major League Cricket (MLC). He also discussed cricket's rapid growth in America and his dream of one day representing the USA.

title: 'Decisions as swift as T20 match': PM Modi bats for deeper India-Australia ties
content: Prime Minister Modi highlighted India-Australia ties using cricket analogies during his visit. He stated that cricket serves as a diplomatic language between the two nations. Both countries will host major sporting events in the coming years. Terrorist attacks in Pahalgam and Sydney were strongly condemned by both leaders. A landmark atomic cooperation agreement was also announced by Prime Minister Modi.

title: Vienna's housing playbook: What rents look like inside Austria's capital
content: Vienna maintains affordable housing through a century-old social model. This system provides homes for half the city's population. Middle-income residents are included, preventing social segregation. Tenants enjoy open-ended contracts and no upfront deposit fees. The city retains public ownership and continues expanding supply.

title: Hack of the Day: How to secure your online accounts with passkeys
content: Passkeys offer a modern and secure alternative to traditional passwords for digital accounts.  These keys use your device's biometrics or screen lock for authentication.  Setting up passkeys requires compatible hardware and browsers for enhanced security.  Logging into accounts becomes a quick process using your fingerprint or face scan.  This method significantly reduces the risk of phishing attacks and password theft.

title: 'Egypt were robbed': NYC mayor Mamdani joins VAR row over Argentina's dramatic comeback
content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani voiced support for Egypt after their World Cup loss. He stated that the African team was "robbed" during their match against Argentina. This remark quickly gained attention amid ongoing officiating debates. Egypt's players and fans also alleged unfair refereeing decisions favoured Argentina. FIFA's head of refereeing defended the VAR interventions and warned against unfounded claims.

title: CJP announces Parliament march on July 20; asks students, parents to join in
content: In a statement issued on Thursday (July 9) morning, the CJP said its Parliament march on July 20 would begin from Jantar Mantar along with educator and climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike since June 28.

title: CPI, CPI(M) oppose TVK government’s move to provide jobs to kin of Karur stampede victims
content: CPI(M) State secretary P. Shanmugam said that with an inquiry into the stampede under way and the matter pending before the court, the decision to offer government jobs could weaken the legal process

title: Sharad Pawar's meeting in Shinde's office sparks tension, war of words between allies Sena (UBT) and NCP (SP)
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut said his party was disturbed and hurt with Sharad Pawar's meeting in Eknath Shinde's office, and stressed that such acts lower the credibility of a senior leader like him

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay to distribute govt. job appointment orders to kin of 32 Karur stampede victims
content: CM Vijay will also lay the foundation stone for Evervan Kothari Footwear Private Limited, which will come up at Manavasi near Krishnarayapuram with an investment of ₹1,700 crore

title: Labourers shifted from tunnel road construction site in Kozhikode in Kerala
content: Revenue department officials say shifting following preventive evacuation measures recommended by Kerala government in the wake of Wayanad debris slip disaster

content: Prabhas' wife also declined to claim the body, alleging that she had endured a lot of abuse from her husband

title: Child with Chandipura virus infection dies in Gujarat's Sabarkantha
content: CHPV is a member of the Rhabdoviridae family and is known to cause sporadic cases and outbreaks of acute encephalitis syndrome

title: Ex-forest officials, conservationists challenge functioning of India’s apex Wildlife Board in Delhi HC
content: The petitioners argue that the National Board for Wildlife and its Standing Committee (SC-NBWL) have become a ‘clearing house’ that routinely diverts protected areas to roads, mining, industry and other non-conservation projects

title: Karnataka government to take steps to stop fake news
content: He said that ‘fake news is causing several problems in society, including causing law and order disturbances’

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | Heavy monsoon rain wreaks havoc across India, disrupts normal life
content: IMD said that there has been a delay of a day in the monsoon, covering the whole country, as it normally takes place on July 8

title: CM Vijay conducts surprise inspection at Govt. Hospital for Women and Children in Egmore
content: At the century-old institute, Mr. Vijay visited the ward and the intensive care unit and enquired about the health of mothers undergoing treatment at the hospital

title: How undercover agents and informants helped the FBI unravel Lawrence Bishnoi’s gang | Explained
content: A years-long U.S. investigation culminated in charges against 37 people linked to alleged transnational crime syndicates. Here’s how the FBI pieced together the case

title: Trauma of giving birth in jail not bearable: Court on bail to TCS staffer Nida Khan
content: The Judge allowed Nida Khan’s plea, considering the defence argument that she was five months pregnant

title: Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan must resign, NTA should be disbanded: Congress
content: Congress attacked the Modi government over the alleged irregularities in the UGC-NET exam, demanding that Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan must resign and the NTA disbanded

title: Action will be taken in Ram temple donation theft case: U.P. BJP chief
content: Pankaj Chaudhary says both State and Central Governments are ensuring that the matter is investigated thoroughly

title: Motorists cautioned after crack found on Thamarassery mountain pass linking Kozhikode and Wayanad in Kerala
content: Wayanad Ghat Road Protection Committee members say all vehicles passing through the ghat section should comply with the speed limit regulations to avoid accidents

title: Vikram Solar to invest ₹15,037 crore in BESS facility at Gangaikondan SIPCOT in Tirunelveli
content: The MoU was signed between the Industries, Investment Promotion and Commerce Department and Vikram Solar Limited in the presence of Tamil Nadu CM C. Joseph Vijay at the Secretariat

title: PM Modi in Australia LIVE: PM Modi highlights India's growth, global trust at Melbourne diaspora event
content: PM Modi announced a key nuclear energy agreement to enable uranium supply from Australia to India after discussions with Australian Prime Minister Albanese

title: Ensure 100% spending of ₹20,643-crore SC Sub-Plan by January: Minister Dola Sree Bala
content: The Minister said the State government had earmarked ₹20,643 crore for the SC Sub-Plan during the current financial year, and instructed every department to prepare detailed action plans to ensure timely and effective utilisation of the funds

title: Retain Visakhapatnam airport for domestic flights after Bhogapuram opens: CPI(M)
content: The party’s district secretary M. Jaggu Naidu asked the MP and MLAs to ensure domestic services to cities such as Mumbai, Delhi and Hyderabad continue from Visakhapatnam even after Bhogapuram becomes operational

title: BJP pushes for ‘safest and shortest’ third route to Amarnath from Ladakh’s Drass
content: The all-weather Zojila Tunnel has opened a new pathway to an alternative route to the Amarnath shrine from Drass district; it should be a 5km trek from Minamarg, says senior BJP leader Tashi Gyalson

title: Modi government hurt national interests by letting China capture India’s critical industries: Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge cited a media report, which claimed that China still powers India’s strategic industries, to attack the government

title: WhatsApp's reply on notice over 'username' feature due July 9: IT Secretary
content: WhatsApp had sought some more time to submit its response on the 'username' feature and had assured the government that it would not roll it out in India until discussions are complete

title: KAAPA accused BJP councillor R. Sugathan granted one-day interim bail for swearing-in
content: However, a final call on his swearing-in will be taken only after Kerala High Court’s verdict on his bail plea in the KAAPA case

title: Income Tax Department opposes same-sex couple’s plea for tax exemption on gifts
content: Bombay High Court told Section 56(2)(x) exemption applies only to relationships recognised as marriage under Indian law; department seeks dismissal of petition

title: Around 3,000 HPCL gas cylinders swept away into Patalganga river amid heavy floods in Raigad
content: Raigad District Collector Kishan Jawale urged people not to retrieve or handle any LPG cylinders found in the river or along its banks

title: India-Australia relationship has ‘never been more consequential’: PM Albanese
content: The leaders will meet in Melbourne for bilateral discussions and PM Modi will also call on Governor General Sam Mostyn AC during the visit

title: Madras High Court stays Look Out Circular issued against former DMK Minister E.V. Velu
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan grants the interim stay on the condition that he must appear for inquiry before the DVAC investigating officer on July 15, 2026, after returning from Singapore on July 12, 2026

title: PM Modi invites Australian businesses to invest in India, seeks early conclusion of CECA
content: Over the past few years, we have built a strong framework for future cooperation by combining the capabilities of both countries, said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Champat Rai not upset about stepping down, supportive of new system: Ram temple trust treasurer
content: “Champat Rai is an old veteran who has devoted his life to the country. He is fully supportive of the new (temple management) system being put in place and he isn’t upset or angry,” Govind Dev Giri said

title: Congress will not rest till Education Minister Pradhan resigns: Rahul Gandhi on NEET paper leak row
content: He shared a video of students protesting in Hyderabad, highlighting the NEET paper leak and demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation

title: Kochi Corporation’s new office may need another ₹4 crore to complete pending works
content: The structure has already incurred ₹61 crore after more than 200% cost escalation over the original estimate of ₹18.83 crore. Technical sanction was granted in 2006. The project was delayed by nearly two decades before being inaugurated on October 21, 2025

title: Bengal government orders probe into hospital administering ‘expired’ saline to patient
content: Health Minister Sharadwat Mukherjee said a report has been sought from the Midnapore Medical College and Hospital in this regard, and assured that corrective measures would be put in place

title: Srikakulam to get airport under UDAN scheme, says Civil Aviation Minister Rammohan Naidu
content: The Union Civil Aviation Minister said the airport in Mandasa mandal would make Srikakulam a mini aviation hub; the CPI(M) opposed it, calling it a threat to the Uddanam cashew and coconut belt

title: Pinarayi Vijayan defends tunnel project clearances; blames  administrative negligence for Wayanad disaster
content: Pinarayi Vijayan alleges that despite Wayanad experiencing heavy rainfall two days prior to the tragedy, the District Disaster Management Authority failed to issue a timely red alert

title: Trump says 11 jets downed during India-Pakistan hostilities last year
content: India has consistently denied any third-party intervention; Mr. Trump claimed the conflict between India and Pakistan “was going to be nuclear”; without providing details, the U.S. President said that “11 planes were shot down” during the conflict

title: Kerala Forest department sets up ‘classroom libraries’ in Madatharakani Government High School
content: 14 libraries, set up right inside individual classrooms, have age-appropriate and curriculum-aligned books within arm’s reach of the children

title: Navy’s Drishti-10 UAV crashes near Gujarat’s Porbandar; inquiry ordered
content: No reports of injuries or casualties on the ground; the mishap is the second involving the Hermes-900-based platform in about 18 months; the crash is likely to renews scrutiny of the UAV’s reliability

title: Gujarat launches first Data Centre Policy, targets ₹6 lakh crore investments and 7.5 GW capacity
content: The policy, launched by Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel in Gandhinagar, offers a wide range of fiscal and non-fiscal incentives, including capital subsidy, interest subsidy, power tariff support, reimbursement of State GST (SGST), exemption from stamp duty and registration charges, and financial assistance for captive desalination plants to meet the sector’s high water requirements

title: BRICS key to advancing women's leadership: Minister Annapurna Devi
content: Investment in women's empowerment is not just a question of social justice, but also a mandatory condition for inclusive and sustainable development

title: Manipur CM invokes Bible, Buddha’s teachings to pitch for peace in Kuki and Naga areas
content: Khemchand Singh visits Kangpokpi and Senapati, assures justice in killing of church leaders and calls for reconciliation among communities

title: Watch: What India thinks of the Ayodhya case
content: The Ayodhya Ram Mandir donations embezzlement case is ongoing and still making headlines. With the Uttar Pradesh elections due next year, how much will this case impact polls. What are the people saying? To understand this better, we are joined by psephologist Yashwant Deshmukh, founder of CVOTER Foundation.

title: Produce Him Physically: Court On Plea To Let Sena Man Appear Via Video Conferencing
content: Rakesh Mhatre was arrested on Wednesday for assaulting two doctors and other staff at a hospital in Dombivli.

title: Southwest Monsoon, Delayed By A Day, Covers Entire Country: Weather Office
content: The department said that there has been a delay of a day in the monsoon, covering the whole country, as it normally takes place on July 8.

content: The accident took place around 3.30 am near Bharula village within the jurisdiction of Civil Lines police station.

content: The issue of counterfeit currency was raised in the Assam Legislative Assembly on Thursday during the fourth day of the Budget Session.

content: At the centre of the protests is the demand to abolish 12 legislative assembly seats reserved for Kashmiri refugees settled in Pakistan.

content: "During Operation Sindoor, you must have seen the explosions that struck terrorist hideouts. Those explosions have resonated across the world," he said.

content: PM Modi concluded a high-level summit with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese earlier today, marking an expansion of the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (CSP).

content: Elon Musk's SpaceXAI is turning up the heat in the AI race, claiming its latest model is closing in on Anthropic.

content: As India and Bangladesh navigate a transitional phase in their relationship, Siam's appointment and posting in India will be significant.

content: India's path to net zero is unlikely to be an either-or choice between electric vehicles and biofuels. It will require both, as per experts.

content: Australia has around 28 per cent of the world's uranium resources, but it doesn't use any nuclear power or weapons and exports all of it.

content: To complement the government’s efforts, the guidelines seek to leverage corporate social responsibility funds along with participation of non-governmental organisations and educational institutions to protect water bodies

content: Though there were people on the platform at the time, a major disaster was narrowly averted as no one was directly beneath the structure when it gave way

title: Youth Congress workers storm Kerala PSC office over alleged exam irregularities
content: Despite attempts by security staff to stop them, the activists tried to force open the door of meeting room and shouted slogans demanding resignation of PSC chairman

title: In Visakhapatnam Central Jail, learning skills for life away from crime
content: The first-of-its-kind digital programme in Andhra Pradesh jails is steering remand prisoners toward meaningful employment after serving prison time. Primarily targeting individuals booked under the NDPS Act, the initiative focuses on rehabilitation and future financial independence of the inmates, explores Harish Gilai

title: State Election Commissioners face controversy in Kerala and Tamil Nadu
content: Controversies in Kerala and Tamil Nadu highlight concerns over the integrity and autonomy of the State Election Commission

title: How Jaswant Singh Khalra uncovered police brutality in Punjab, and what price he paid for it
content: With Satluj bringing back the spotlight on state violence on youth of Punjab during 1990s, here is why Jaswant Singh Khalra remains a revered figure even today

title: TTE suspended for allowing unauthorised decorator hired by a couple into First AC coach
content: A couple travelling in a First AC coach of Nandigram Express had hired a decorator through an online platform to adorn their compartment during the journey on July 6

title: From village library to a window on world cinema
content: Kolazhy’s ‘Cinema Kottaka’ celebrates its 100th screening, marking a decade of bringing world cinema to rural audiences with Malayalam subtitles

title: Minister Nirmalkumar’s claims on Parandur site are false propaganda: T.N. BJP
content: Nainar Nagenthran charged that the TVK-led government under Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has undertaken steps to stall the economic development of Tamil Nadu and the employment prospects of the youth of the State.
